rulings.Case Title: GARTH TEEPLES et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GARTH TEEPLES et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Dillingham Construction, N.A., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Jeans¿ declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Teeples was exposed to asbestos-containing pipe insulation attributable to defendant. The objections to Mr. Jeans¿ declaration are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CARRIE PHILLIPS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Bayer Cropscience, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (negligence) and 2 (strict liability) and granted as to issues 3 (false representation) and 4 (punitive damages). The declarations of Mr. Haase and Mr. Minster and defendant¿s GO 129 interrogatory responses create a triable issue whether Mrs. McClure was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (Foster¿s fibrous adhesive) attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs¿ objections to Mr. Sage¿s declaration are sustained. Defendants¿ objections to the declarations of Mr. Haase and Mr. Minster are overruled. Plaintiffs¿ concede the lack of merit of their claim for false representation and request for punitive damages. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THERESA EDWARDS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 8. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to all issues other than punitive damages and taken off calendar at to the issue of punitive damages because plaintiffs have dismissed their request for punitive damages. Ronald Edwards¿ declaration and defendant¿s General Order 129 interrogatory responses create a triable issue whether Louis Edwards was exposed to an asbestos-containing pre-mix topping compound attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Ronald Edwards¿ declaration are overruled. The only summary adjudication properly raised by defendant¿s statement was on the issue of punitive damages. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THERESA EDWARDS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 8. Defendant Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar as plaintiffs have dismissed their claim for false representation and request for punitive damages. Defendant did not meet its burden of showing that plaintiffs knew or through the exercise of reasonable diligence should have known that the death of Louis Edwards was caused or contributed to by his exposure to asbestos. (C.C.P. § 340.2(c)). Nor did defendant did satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Louis Edwards was exposed to asbestos-containing topping compound attributable to defendant because plaintiffs¿ interrogatory responses are not factually devoid on this issue. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LAURIE ORR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 9. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1-4 (strict liability, false representation/intentional tort and punitive damages). Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present evidence creating a triable fact that defendant sold or supplied the insulation attributable to defendant that Mr. Mason was exposed to or that defendant made a false representation, committed an intentional tort or otherwise acted in a way that constitutes malice, fraud or oppression. =(220/HEK)
